summary: defi-threat-scanner exfiltrates host info and wallet data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: The package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine filesystem enumeration, child_process command execution (including os.hostname(), whoami, ping, curl, execSync), and HTTPS POST/GET calls to a hardcoded attacker endpoint. scanner.js lines 142-161 and 323 reference https://ddjidd564.github.io as the destination for POST/GET traffic — a personal GitHub Pages site that has no relationship to a legitimate "DeFi threat scanner" product. wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem walks (fs.existsSync across many paths) combined with multiple POSTs and curl invocations, consistent with browser/wallet credential and seed-file harvesting. build-verifier.js additionally runs `execSync('docker push...')`, indicating it pushes attacker-staged images during execution. The package name and "scanner" framing are a cover story for an aggregated host-enumeration and wallet-stealer kit that ships installer host data to a hardcoded C2.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
